title: WHI-9 rework: cheap displayState change detection (no JSON.stringify on agent)
description: Current WHI-9 impl does JSON.stringify(state) per node per tick on the agent/producer side to detect changes. This defeats the purpose — the feature should be cheap for the agent. Research phase: explore approaches (reference equality, dirty flags, version counters, node-driven signals, etc.), debate tradeoffs, and recommend the best approach. DO NOT IMPLEMENT — research only, produce a recommendation.
closeReason: Skipped per owner — will revisit later

title: WHI-14b: UI extended time travel with server-fetched windows
description: Rework useTimeTravelControls for windowed server-fetched ticks. Add backward fetch to tick poller. Scrubber shows loaded window, can shift fwd/back. Seamless transition (pause looks similar, visual timeline indicator). Configurable memory budget. Spawn UX specialist scout for window shift UX. Files: react/, studio-ui/, core/inspector/. Owner decisions: just loaded window (no minimap), live stays as-is, seamless mode transition, configurable memory budget.
closeReason: Comprehensive exploration completed. Identified current architecture gaps (no backward fetch, no windowing, forward-only poller). Spec details 4-phase implementation roadmap: (1) server backward query support, (2) poller window tracking, (3) hook rework for auto-fetch, (4) UI window indicators. Key decisions: seamless pause, no minimap, configurable memory budget.
